experiment.title: Twelve Labs Marengo 3.0 & Pegasus 1.2 Evaluation on Worker Safety Video Classification
experiment.objective: Independently benchmark Twelve Labs video foundation models on an industrial safety dataset to assess fitness for production safety monitoring

evaluation_subset.note: 22 videos failed to index and 20 failed Pegasus labeling due to API rate limits (8 req/min on free tier)

pipeline: 1. Download dataset from HuggingFace via FiftyOne (load_from_hub)
2. Filter test split for videos >= 4s duration
3. Stratified sample up to 10 videos per label (seed=42)
4. Create TwelveLabs index with both marengo3.0 and pegasus1.2
5. Upload and index each video via tasks.create + tasks.wait_for_done
6. Retrieve 512-dim Marengo embeddings via indexes.videos.retrieve (multi-segment averaged)
7. Classify each video via Pegasus analyze endpoint with constrained prompt
8. Run text-to-video search queries via search.query endpoint
9. Compute metrics: kNN-CV, silhouette, mAP@5, recall@5, accuracy, format compliance

generation_evaluation.prompt_strategy: Constrained label set with explicit safe/unsafe grouping
generation_evaluation.prompt:
You are a worker-safety video classifier. Classify this video into exactly one label.

UNSAFE:
- Safe Walkway Violation
- Unauthorized Intervention
- Opened Panel Cover
- Carrying Overload with Forklift

SAFE:
- Safe Walkway
- Authorized Intervention
- Closed Panel Cover
- Safe Carrying

Return ONLY the exact label name. No punctuation, no explanation, no extra words.

rate_limiting.mitigation: Proactive 10s interval between calls + retry with parsed retry-after header
rate_limiting.impact: 22 videos failed indexing, 20 failed Pegasus labeling due to exhausted retries

benchmark_a_embedding_quality.interpretation: Embeddings carry weak class signal (random baseline=0.125) but clusters heavily overlap (silhouette near 0). Binary safe/unsafe barely better than chance.

benchmark_b_retrieval_quality.interpretation: Strong at object-level retrieval (forklift, panel, equipment). Fails completely at state discrimination (open vs closed, inside vs outside walkway, authorized vs unauthorized).

Authorized Intervention.note: All 10 failed due to rate limits

Carrying Overload with Forklift.note: All 7 failed due to rate limits

critical_failure_modes: Pegasus returned 'Unsafe' (not a valid label) 57% of the time — defaults to binary judgment instead of 8-class classification
Safe Walkway Violation classified as 'Safe Walkway' in 2/3 cases — misses the violation entirely
Carrying Overload with Forklift classified as 'Safe Carrying' in prior run — calls dangerous behavior safe
Closed Panel Cover (safe) classified as 'Unsafe' — false alarm on correct behavior
60% hallucination rate despite explicit prompt constraints and temperature=0.1

marengo_strengths: Object-level retrieval works well (forklift AP=1.0, equipment AP=0.89)
512-dim embeddings are compact and practical for downstream ML
Multi-segment temporal encoding provides video-level representations
37-language support and 4-hour video capacity are production-grade specs
marengo_weaknesses: Cannot discriminate states: open vs closed, inside vs outside boundary, authorized vs unauthorized
Walkway queries returned AP=0.0 — no understanding of spatial boundary compliance
Panel cover state (open vs closed) completely indistinguishable
Silhouette score of 0.024 means safety classes are not meaningfully separated in embedding space
Benchmarks published by Twelve Labs are sports-focused; no published safety/industrial benchmarks
pegasus_strengths: Can identify visually distinct safe behaviors (Safe Carrying: 67% recall)
pegasus_weaknesses: 60% hallucination rate on constrained classification — returns invalid labels
Defaults to 'Unsafe' as a catch-all (57% of responses) instead of specific labels
Cannot distinguish violation from compliance on the same activity (walkway violation → 'Safe Walkway')
No structured output / constrained decoding mode — freeform text output only
Ignores explicit prompt instructions ('Return ONLY the exact label name')
production_concerns: Rate limit of 8 req/min on free tier makes batch evaluation impractical — 42% of our samples failed
API-only deployment precludes edge/air-gapped industrial environments
The walkway violation misclassification (unsafe → safe) represents liability risk in real deployments
No fine-tuning available — cannot adapt models to domain-specific safety categories

commands: python eval_harness.py --split test --samples-per-label 3   # quick sanity check (24 videos)
python eval_harness.py --split test --samples-per-label 10  # full run (72+ videos)
notes: First run downloads ~9.5GB dataset from HuggingFace
State is persisted to eval_state.json — interrupted runs resume without re-indexing
Free tier rate limits severely constrain batch runs; expect ~20% failure rate on large batches
Results may vary slightly due to Pegasus non-determinism even at temperature=0.1
